sections.paragraph:
-

(1) prop. i.q. Arab نزع to pull up, to pluck out (compare נָסַח); e.g. door posts, Jud. 16:3 , 14 Jdg 16:14especially the stakes of a tent when a camp moves, Isa 33:20. Hence-

(2) to remove a camp, to break up from an encampment (as a nomadic band), Gen 35:16 37:17 Num 10:18 33:3 , seqq; an army of soldiers, Exo 14:10 2Ki 19:8 and also-

(3) to remove, to depart (aufbrechen); e.g. used of the angel of God, Exo 14:19 also used of the ark of the covenant, Num 10:33 of a wind springing up, Num 11:31 to migrate, to journey, often used of nomadic tribes, Gen 12:9 33:17 .

(4) to bend a bow; Arab. نزع; see מַסַּע .

Niphal, to be plucked out, used of the cords of a tent, Job 4:21 (see under the word יֶתֶר ); of a tent itself, Isa 38:12.

Hiphil הִסִּיעַ-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 2; to cause a camp to remove, Exo 15:22 Psa 78:26.

(2) causat. of Kal No. 3, to cause to go, to lead, Psa 78:52 also to take away, 2Ki 4:4.

(3) to pluck up as a tree, Job 19:10 a vine, Psa 80:9 to cut out (to quarry) stones, Ecc 10:9 1 Ki. 5:31 .

Derivatives, מַסַּע, מַסָּע.
